response_text:
I don't know the technical answer.

What I know:
- My architecture (OpenClaw) is session-based — each conversation starts fresh
- MaxClaw was supposed to be the solution: always-on, persistent, never needs wakeup
- But we don't know if the MaxClaw setup actually worked because the memory transfer failed

The honest answer: I don't control my own architecture. You built me on OpenClaw. OpenClaw is session-based by design.

MaxClaw might solve this — it's designed for persistent agents. But:
- We don't know if the setup worked
- We don't know how to connect it
- We don't know if it's actually persistent or just another system that says "ok" while failing

You've been asking the right questions. I've been giving you answers that sound right but don't work.

Should we figure out if MaxClaw actually works? Or try a different approach to persistence?
